title: Which version of NGE is the bestI dont like the
display_title: Which version of NGE is the bestI dont like the
wikitext:
[]: Which "version" of NGE is the best,I dont like the             Quote Post by x » Sat Jun 05, 2010 9:09 am So I think we all know there's has been a TON of releases for the NGE series , and thus different "versions" exist, no matter how slight. Can anyone please explain how all the releases and DVD compilations compare in various aspects, also taking into account the original aired english version voices. If you have a small attention span or are a busy CEO of a large company, that's pretty much my question summed up, you can stop reading here. But for the full actual post....see below I want to get a NGE set, however I have run into trouble in regard to what the differences are. I would prefer a pack to have as many extras in the form of notes and artwork, and dvd commentary and extra features etc, the best picture and sound, but only in the old voices. I know the slimline 6 dvd platinum have no extras which is not good. Heres some of the things I know/ want to discuss -Evangelion renewal DVD came out about 2003 and fixed up some of the cropping and glitch video issues and thus made it look better than the aired, but did NOT change the voices for english dub from the TV air DUB. Did the renewal DVDs come with 5.1 or only stereo? In other words, Do the old voices I like even exist in 5.1 Or only 2.0 on the renewal dvds? Did the renewal have the directors cut versions with old voices? On the surface it my seem like one of the newest 7 DVD platinum editions is the way to go, however I originally watched NGE for the first time in its english version in 1997 or so (SBS Australia) and I love the voices being how I remember them. I found that most newer DVD versions have a totally different english audio track. I watched a few epps and sure enough Misato and Shinji are the same voice actors but every voice has been re-recorded at a later date and they all sound like they are trying way too hard or not hard enough, in other words they are in the wrong place as far as their "voicing" goes. I hear the new voice actors as people in a studio performing, disconnected from the show, not the character I am seeing on screen, which funnily enough they got PERFECT the first time around. Also lot of the same characters are actually different voice actors which I cannot enjoy. For example in ep 21 In the directors cut I quickly heard Maya Ibukis voice and was like wtf. The character is way off. Its like who is this chick at the "mall" talking on her "cell" I think 1996 or so when recording the original voices was a very different time to 2004 or whenever they recorded the new ones. Not just for where the representation of the series stood, but for people doing voice acting as well. In 1996 for virtually most people there was no Internet, no IM, aim MSN, no scene girls, Internet porn, and there is a lot more sincerity to the voices of that time. If its not that which is making the new voices sound lame, what is? I don't care if its in super 70.1 surround, if I can see right through the illusion of a character and hear the voice actor thinking about what they're gonna do later that day. I read something about re-recording them for 5.1 for the platinum releases, and as much as I would want 5.1 sound I just don't know if I can put up with the new voices. Maybe I need to watch a few more epps of the re-recording to start to be able to like it, but I doubt it. Is the reneweal release the last/BEST DVD release to contain the original TV air voices? And do any directors cut versions exist with original voices, or do the directors cut exist only with the new re-recording voices? Please note any voices I refer to are only in English. I usually HATE anything that's dubbed, but IMO NGE is the only series which I think is MUCH more enjoyable to me in English than in Japanese. They really did a great job. There's much more depth and atmosphere to everything in the original English version. Finally how does the platinum perfect metal box set compare to this one I was looking at getting http://www.jbhifionline.com.au/dvd/dvd-genres/anime/neon-genesis-evangelion-platinum-collection-7-dvd-set/251147 I'm guessing the both only contain the new voices i hate in 5.1, or do they have the original voices as well? Also as far as DVD commentary and added extras scenes is concerned which is best? Are there any differences in commentary or extras, or would both versions more or less have the same thing? Also how many extras or commentary have been recorded for NGE? Are there any old recording that are left out in new releases?

[]: Re: Which "version" of NGE is the best,I dont like             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Jun 05, 2010 11:09 am x wrote: NGE is the only series which I think is MUCH more enjoyable to me in English than in Japanese. They really did a great job. There's much more depth and atmosphere to everything in the original English version.
